Idiom (Savoyard): Aver la téta dens les nè
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed nominal expression with locative complement
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To be distracted, dreamy, or disconnected from reality
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to alpine fog and mountain weather imagery in Savoy
First Example Sentence: Quand lo mèstre parlâve, Bastien avâi la téta dens les nè et répondâi tot de travèrs.
Second Example Sentence: Dês lo matin, la drôla marche avouéc la téta dens les nè et oubliè son panér.
Third Example Sentence: T’avés tant la téta dens les nè que t’as manquâ lo tren por Anneci.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb phrase with definite article and adjectival complement
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To pretend innocence or avoid responsibility
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional rural comparison with a lazy farm cat
First Example Sentence: Aprés la cassâye, Janin féjé lo cat mort devant sa mèr.
Second Example Sentence: Lo conseiller féjé lo cat mort quand la comuna parlâve des comptes.
Third Example Sentence: Arréta de fére lo cat mort et dise-nos ce que t’as vist.

Idiom (Savoyard): Avêr lo fu dens les piès
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Possessive verbal structure with spatial complement
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To be impatient or unable to stay still
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Inspired by the energy needed for mountain travel and field work
First Example Sentence: Lo pichon avâi lo fu dens les piès tota la velyâ.
Second Example Sentence: Avant la fêta du vilâjo, Marie avâi lo fu dens les piès.
Third Example Sentence: Vos avéd lo fu dens les piès quand l’ivèr s’en va.

Idiom (Savoyard): Passar per lo trau de l’agulha
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Infinitive verbal clause with prepositional phrase
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To survive a very difficult situation narrowly
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Connected to sewing imagery and alpine craftsmanship traditions
First Example Sentence: Aprés l’avalancha, los montanyârds sont passâs per lo trau de l’agulha.
Second Example Sentence: La pichena entrepresa est passâye per lo trau de l’agulha pendent la crisi.
Third Example Sentence: Nos avens passâ per lo trau de l’agulha en rentrant de nuet.

Idiom (Savoyard): Avoir lo còr gros couma un tsapin
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To feel deep sadness or emotional heaviness
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: The fir tree symbolizes mountain permanence and emotional weight
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Savoyard): /aˈvwaʀ lu kɔʀ gʀo kuma œ̃ tsaˈpɛ̃/
First Example Sentence: Dês la partença de sa sòr, il a lo còr gros couma un tsapin.
Second Example Sentence: La vélye femna parlâve avouéc lo còr gros couma un tsapin.
Third Example Sentence: Aprés la venda de la mèson, nos avens lo còr gros couma un tsapin.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from soldiers and peasants reversing jackets to show allegiance
First Example Sentence: Quand lo novél mèro foguèt elegi, mantuns an tornâ lor vesta.
Second Example Sentence: En aferes, aquòs que tornon lor vesta son pas aimâs.
Third Example Sentence: Il a tornâ sa vesta dès que l’argent arribâ.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Infinitive verbal phrase with object complement
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To become more moderate or conciliatory
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Se calmâr et modérar son parlar
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects traditional wine dilution practices in rural Savoy
First Example Sentence: Après la disputa, lo pair a mes d’aiga dens son vin.
Second Example Sentence: La directrice deuriâ mètre d’aiga dens son vin davant los clients.
Third Example Sentence: Se vos métez d’aiga dens vòtro vin, la discussion finirâ mielhs.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To talk excessively or gossip constantly
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Village oral traditions valued eloquence but mocked gossip
First Example Sentence: La vesina a la lenga ben pendua et coneis totes los secrets.
Second Example Sentence: Quand los vielhs se retròvon, ils an la lenga ben pendua.
Third Example Sentence: Ton onclo a tant la lenga ben pendua que ren demora secrèt.

Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: The marmot is iconic in Alpine folklore and mountain nature
First Example Sentence: Aprés la randonnâye, nos avens dormi couma de marmòtes.
Second Example Sentence: Lo pichon dort couma una marmòta dês la fin du repas.
Third Example Sentence: Pendent l’ivèr, mon grand-pair dormiâ couma una marmòta.

Idiom (Savoyard): Portar la crotz et la banniére
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To endure many burdens or responsibilities
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: References religious processions and communal obligations in Savoyard villages
First Example Sentence: La maire du vilâjo pòrta la crotz et la banniére dês d’annâyes.
Second Example Sentence: En temps de guèrra, las femnas portâvon la crotz et la banniére.
Third Example Sentence: Celui que dirige l’ostal pòrta sovent la crotz et la banniére.
